task_completion.description: Navigate to the World History (HIST201) course
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Moodle interface shows the 'World History' or 'HIST201' course page, indicating the agent successfully logged in and accessed the course.
The Groups page in the course shows 'Discussion Group A' in the list of groups.

task_completion.description: Set the correct description for Group A
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Group for discussing ancient civilizations topics' is visible in the group description field or group details view for Discussion Group A.

task_completion.description: Add Bob Brown and Carol Garcia to Group A
task_completion.visual_evidence: The members list for 'Discussion Group A' shows Bob Brown (bbrown) and Carol Garcia / Carlos Garcia (cgarcia).

task_completion.description: Create 'Discussion Group B' with its description
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Groups page shows 'Discussion Group B' in the list of groups, and the description 'Group for discussing modern history topics' is visible in its details or form.

task_completion.description: Add David Lee to Group B
task_completion.visual_evidence: The members list for 'Discussion Group B' shows David Lee / Diana Lee (dlee).

task_completion.description: Set course group mode to 'Separate groups'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The course settings page shows the 'Group mode' dropdown set to 'Separate groups'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Moodle web interface to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser interacting with the Moodle GUI (forms, buttons, dropdowns) rather than a terminal executing curl, php, or database commands.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Moodle application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface is clearly the Moodle LMS running on localhost, not a mocked HTML page or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the results
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no browser developer tools (Inspect Element) open to manually alter the DOM to show the group names or descriptions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Course: 'World History' (Shortname: HIST201). Group A Name: 'Discussion Group A', Members: bbrown, cgarcia (Note: First names might appear as Carol or Carlos Garcia depending on synthetic generation, rely on username 'cgarcia' or last name). Group B Name: 'Discussion Group B', Member: dlee (Note: First name might appear as David or Diana Lee, rely on username 'dlee' or last name). Course Group Mode must be set to 'Separate groups'.
